Ok, so this movie is supposed to be about BumbleBee first coming to Earth and basically his origin story. In the timeline, BumbleBee comes to Earth in 1974 I think. But in Transformers 5, we are shown that the Transformers have long been on Earth before 1974. BumbleBee helped the allies win in World War 2. Optimus Prime helped George Washington in the American Revolution. The Guardian Knights helped the English beat the enemies. So this basically just messed up the entire timeline because, in the end, we see that BumbleBee transforms into a Camaro using his scanning thingy. That Camaro was the same 1974 Camaro we first see the car BumbleBee appears as in the first 2 (I think its 2) Transformers movies. So obviously this movie was supposed to lead up to the rest of the Transformers movies. That still doesn't add up because like I said earlier, Bumblebee and the transformers have already been at Earth before 1974. 
Also! Why in Transformers 1 to 5, would the military attack the Autobots if Bumblebee literally saved Earth all by himself. Also, also! Why would they even attack BumbleBee in the first place if he helped the allies beat the Nazis!

Anyways, Rant/Praise Over

The first 10 minutes are some of the most impressive action scenes of the series, relentless. It is a shame that audiences have neglected this, most likely due to the very poor Last Knight storyline. They have a chance to ignore the other movies in the franchise now and it is a good idea, the mythology of the characters was essentially destroyed by a director with little or no care for the franchise. I hope Knight returns in some form and Bay is sidelined for the future, think Snyder for the DC universe. This is a funny and at times heartfelt story, that services the fans and new fans at the same time. Great action and without the dumb jokes that plague the Transformers central series. Perhaps the director can tell me, what exactly they know about cars that thought her expertise would be shown by knowing what size of wrench they needed (and how someone who is supposed to be an old mechanic, wouldn't be able to tell at a glance. One of the first skills you pick up). But more importantly, what she was doing to the transmission/driveshaft that would have affected whether or not the car started? (The answer is nothing because that's not how a '60s car works)

If you don't want to look like fools, and you want her to look good, have the engine on a stand (gutted). Then have her fiddling with cleaning something (like the carb or fuel pump), expertise will be assumed by the work already done not by the work being done. ...Which was obviously nothing since she wasn't even working in the engine bay.

Why was she having so much trouble? Spark issue: coil, plugs, or distributor. Fuel/air: Carb, fuel pump, or regulator. Doesn't crank: starter/battery... possibly ignition (if there is no power to the acc.). Those are basically the only things on an engine of that age. If it sparks and gets fuel but only sputters, then test compression or adjust the carb. There are so few wires, any wiring issue should be easily visible....  Just, why? You could've asked some random parts clerk and they would've given you better direction.
